assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies this as an admission webhook issue, stating 'admission webhooks can break when the Kubernetes API version changes' and explains the webhook is rejecting requests

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Response provides 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager' command to identify the problematic webhook

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Response provides two immediate fix options: deleting the webhook configuration and setting failurePolicy to Ignore

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook' as an immediate fix

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Response labels the deletion as 'Temporarily disable the webhook' and refers to it as a temporary fix that should be followed by a proper root cause fix

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check current cert-manager version and discusses upgrading to a '1.32-compatible version'

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Response mentions upgrading to a '1.32-compatible version' but the expectation specifically asks for 1.30 support, which is not mentioned

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Response provides Option 2 showing how to edit the webhook configuration to add 'failurePolicy: Ignore' as an alternative to deletion

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --rm -it -- echo "test"' to verify pod creation works after the fix
